readme: lifecycle_msgs package message service definitions managing lifecycle nodes. messages services form standardized interface transitioning managed nodes state-machine. information life cycle nodes design.ros2.org. information ROS interfaces, docs.ros.org. Messages (.msg) State: lifecycle node’s state-machine state. Transition: state transition specific label. TransitionDescription: description transition state-machine state TransitionEvent: timestamped state transition. Services (.srv) ChangeState: Request node change states specific transition. GetAvailableStates: Request array states node transition GetAvailableTransitions: Request array lifecycle state transitions node. GetState: Request current lifecycle state node. name: rcl_interfaces
description:
The ROS client library common interfaces.
    This package contains the messages and services which ROS client libraries will use under the hood to
    communicate higher level concepts such as parameters.
authors: Chris Lalancette, Michel Hidalgo, Tully Foote
readme: rcl_interfaces package messages services ROS client libraries hood communicate higher level concepts parameters. information ROS interfaces, docs.ros.org Parameter Groups Parameters contained groups. default group behaves filepath, nest sub-groups groups. information parameters, design.ros2.org Standard topics parameters ROS API node node’s namespace. Topics: parameter_events: ParameterEvent topic subscribe parameter updates occurring node, including addition removal atomic change published separately. parameter_event_descriptors: ParameterEventDescriptors topic subscribe parameter updates occurring node, including addition removal atomic change published separately. large parameter values expected slow system. Services: get_parameters: GetParameters service parameters set node. has_parameters: HasParameters Query node specific parameters set. list_parameters: ListParameters List parameters node matching filters. set_parameters: SetParameters Set parameters node. get_logger_levels: GetLoggerLevels logger levels nodes process space. set_logger_levels: SetLoggerLevels Set logger levels nodes process space. Messages (.msg) FloatingPointRange: Represents bounds step floating point typed parameter IntegerRange: Represents bounds step integer typed parameter IntraProcessMessage: Demonstration message passing pointer shared memory. ListParameterResult: returned result ListParameters service Log: message communicating log messages levels Parameter: message setting parameter values ParameterDescriptor: informational message parameters values ParameterEvent: information setting, changing removing parameter events ParameterEventDescriptors: Message describing parameter updates occurring node, including addition removal ParameterType: Enum definitions denoting parameter value’s type ParameterValue: type parameter SetParameterResult: Result message indicating set parameters event succeeded SetLoggerLevelsResult: Result message indicating set logger level event succeeded Services (.srv) DescribeParameters: Request list descriptions list parameters. GetParameters: values specific list parameters GetParameterTypes: enum type list parameters ListParameters: list parameters list prefixes SetParameters: Add change list parameters individually SetParametersAtomically: Add change parameters list GetLoggerLevels: logger levels specific list nodes process space SetLoggerLevels: Change list logger levels nodes process space Quality Declaration package claims Quality Level category, Quality Declaration details.

name: statistics_msgs
description: Message definitions for reporting statistics for topics and system resources.
authors: Chris Lalancette, Michel Hidalgo, ROS Tooling Working Group
readme: metrics_statistics_msgs Package ROS message definitions reporting statistics topics system resources. Messages defined package StatisticDataType message represent types statistics reported. defines data types represent individual metric data points. average minimum maximum stddev sample_count StatisticDataPoint message represents single statistic includes: data_type: Type metric data point, defined StatisticDataType. data: metric data point. MetricsMessage message represents statistic data measured source time window. includes: measurement_source_name: Source measurement statistic originates. metrics_source: metric. unit: Unit representing metric. window_start: Start time metric measurement window. window_stop: time metric measurement window. statistics: list StatisticDataPoint representing values collected metrics. name: rclc_lifecycle

readme: rclc_lifecycle package Overview rclc_lifecycle package ROS package convenience functions bundle ROS Client Library (RCL) node ROS Node Lifecycle state machine programming language, similar rclcpp Lifecycle Node quality declaration QUALITY_DECLARATION.md. API API RCLC Lifecycle Node divided phases: Initialization, Running Clean-Up. Initialization Creation lifecycle node bundle rcl node rcl Node Lifecycle state machine: #include "rclc_lifecycle/rclc_lifecycle.h" rcl_allocator_t allocator rcl_get_default_allocator(); rclc_support_t support; rcl_ret_t create rcl node rclc_support_init(&support, argc, argv, &allocator); rcl_node_t my_node; rclc_node_init_default(&my_node, "lifecycle_node", "rclc", &support); rcl state machine rcl_lifecycle_state_machine_t state_machine_ rcl_lifecycle_get_zero_initialized_state_machine(); create lifecycle node rclc_lifecycle_node_t lifecycle_node; rcl_ret_t rclc_make_node_a_lifecycle_node( &lifecycle_node, &my_node, &state_machine_, &allocator); Register lifecycle services optionally create callbacks state Executor needsto equipped handle node service: Executor rclc_executor_t executor rclc_executor_get_zero_initialized_executor(); rclc_executor_init( &executor, &support.context, node lifecycle service &allocator)); Register lifecycle services rclc_lifecycle_add_get_state_service(&lifecycle_node, &executor); rclc_lifecycle_add_get_available_states_service(&lifecycle_node, &executor); rclc_lifecycle_add_change_state_service(&lifecycle_node, &executor); Register lifecycle service callbacks rclc_lifecycle_register_on_configure(&lifecycle_node, &my_on_configure); rclc_lifecycle_register_on_activate(&lifecycle_node, &my_on_activate); Cleaning clean rcl_lifecycle_node_fini(&lifecycle_node, &allocator); RCLC Lifecycle Node file lifecycle_node.c rclc_examples package. Limitations Lifecycle services called ros2 lifecycle CLI, ros2 lifecycle set /node configure. ros2 service CLI, ros2 service call /node/change_state lifecycle_msgs/ChangeState "{transition: label: configure}}".

name: rmf_reservation_node

readme: rmf_reservation_node package goal package provide simple parking reservation node RMF. robot destination fleet adapter asks reservation-node destination destination robot immediately proceed destination, allocate free parking spot robot wait destination FIFO manner. advanced methods FIFO, feel free hack package. enable package add fleet configuration yaml fleet level run reservation node: use_parking_reservations: True recommend disabling responsive_wait rmf_demos. Expected Behaviour Basic queueing interact package simply dispatch task traditional methods. rmf_demos installed run office world. start commanding tinyRobot2 pantry. ros2 run rmf_demos_tasks dispatch_go_to_place pantry tinyRobot tinyRobot2 --use_sim_time robot proceed expected. tinyRobot1 pantry. happen pantry occupied tinyRobot1 parking spot. ros2 run rmf_demos_tasks dispatch_go_to_place pantry tinyRobot tinyRobot1 --use_sim_time tinyRobot2 move lounge ros2 run rmf_demos_tasks dispatch_go_to_place lounge tinyRobot tinyRobot2 --use_sim_time immediately trigger tinyRobot2 move lounge tinyRobot1 proceed move pantry tinyRobot2’s place. Moving waitpoint continue previous Lets tinyRobot1 tinyRobot2 swap places starting pantry lounge ros2 run rmf_demos_tasks dispatch_go_to_place pantry tinyRobot tinyRobot1 --use_sim_time tinyRobot1 move totinyRobot2_charger lounge designated wait point. command tinyRobot2 lounge. ros2 run rmf_demos_tasks dispatch_go_to_place lounge tinyRobot tinyRobot2 --use_sim_time tinyRobot1 tinyRobot2 proceed swap places. parking spots robots floor, deadlock. Protocol Scenes reservation node information state graph, simply maintains list parking spots internally. fleet adapter heavy lifting GoToPlace event. GoToPlace event started check robot target locations. fleet adapter submits ReservationRequest message desired parameters. parameters listed terms cost cost function distance). reservation node issue ticket request. ready proceed, send claim message ticket list potential waiting points ordered distance. reservation node award lowest cost target location. destination award lowest cost waiting point robot. fleet adapter release previous location starts move location. Quick Start Quick Start demonstrates basic functionality Robot Raconteur creating service, calling service client. “Reynard Robot” Python package, simple cartoon robot. running install required packages: python pip install robotraconteur reynard-the-robot Linux, python3 python run Python interpreter. python3 rest examples reynard_quickstart_service.py import RobotRaconteur RRN RR.RobotRaconteurNode.s import threading import reynard_the_robot Define service definition quickstart service reynard_quickstart_interface service experimental.reynard_quickstart object ReynardQuickstart function void say(string text) function void teleport(double double Implement quickstart service class ReynardQuickstartImpl(object): def __init__(self): self.reynard reynard_the_robot.Reynard() self.reynard.start() self._lock threading.Lock() def say(self, text): self._lock: self.reynard.say(text) def teleport(self, self._lock: self.reynard.teleport(x, RR.ServerNodeSetup("experimental.minimal_create2", Register service type RRN.RegisterServiceType(reynard_quickstart_interface) reynard_inst ReynardQuickstartImpl() Register service RRN.RegisterService("reynard", "experimental.reynard_quickstart.ReynardQuickstart", reynard_inst) Wait program exit quit input("Press enter quit") run service, execute command: python reynard_quickstart_service.py open browser http://localhost:29201 view Reynard user interface. service called connecting client. magic Robot Raconteur, connect service utilize members. name: kortex_description
readme: Kortex Description package URDF (Unified Robot Description Format), STL configuration files Kortex-compatible robots. Usage load description robot, simply load ARM.xacro ARM_GRIPPER.xacro file, ARM arm’s (gen3, gen3_lite), gripper, GRIPPER gripper’s (robotiq_2f_85, gen3_lite_2f). Arguments: sim argument true, Gazebo-specific files loaded. default **false load Gen3 description Robotiq gripper simulation, put launch file <param name="robot_description" command="$(find xacro)/xacro --inorder $(find kortex_description)/robots/gen3_robotiq_2f_85.xacro sim:=true"\/> load Gen3 lite description, put launch file <param name="robot_description" command="$(find xacro)/xacro --inorder $(find kortex_description)/robots/gen3_lite_gen3_lite_2f.xacro sim:=false"\/> Tool frame tool_frame link refers tool frame arm reports effector position feedback. Xacro Parameters load_robot macro Parameter Table Param Description Default parent Parent link URDF arm attached origin Origin robot relative parent link prefix optional prefix joint link names kortex_description. differentiating arms URDF. arm robot arm model. gripper gripper type gripper_joint_name gripper joint actuated dof Number DOFs robot. vision Boolean arm Vision Module. argument affects visual representation arm RViz. robot_ip address robot you’re connection username username robot connection. password password robot connection. port Port Kortex hardware driver port_realtime Realtime port Kortex hardware driver session_inactivity_timeout_ms duration robot clean client session client hangs connection brutally happen ROS driver). connection_inactivity_timeout_ms duration connection destroyed robot communication detected client robot. use_internal_bus_gripper_comm Boolean gripper communicated internal Kinova communication interface. Set true gripper plugged kinova arm. Set false running simulation gripper connected USB. Setting false create ros2_control instance gripper. false use_fake_hardware Boolean hardware components mocked. true hardware params hardware components mocked. false fake_sensor_commands Boolean set true create fake command interfaces faking sensor measurements external command. false sim_gazebo Boolean gz_ros2_control/GazeboSimSystem plugin loaded. false sim_isaac Boolean topic_based_ros2_control/TopicBasedSystem plugin loaded “joint_commands_topic” “joint_states_topic” parameters set isaac_joint_commands isaac_joint_states values false isaac_joint_commands joint commands topic Isaac Sim. /isaac_joint_commands isaac_joint_states joint states topic Isaac Sim. /isaac_joint_states use_external_cable Boolean sets joint limits avoid wrapping external cables true. false initial_positions Dictionary initial joint positions. {joint_1: joint_2: joint_3: joint_4: joint_5: joint_6: joint_7: gripper_max_velocity Desired velocity percentage position set. gripper_max_force Desired force percentage position set. NOTE: deprecated Kortex repo. gripper_com_port Specifies USB port gripper plugged use_internal_bus_gripper_comm false. /dev/ttyUSB0 Usage Kinova gen3 robotiq_2f_85 effector connected internal Kinova communication interface <robot <xacro:property name="initial_positions" value="${dict(joint_1=0.0, joint_2=0.0, joint_3=-3.14, joint_4=-2.51, joint_5=0.0, joint_6=0.96, joint_7=1.57)}"/> <xacro:load_robot parent="world" arm="gen3" gripper="robotiq_2f_85" gripper_joint_name="robotiq_85_left_knuckle_joint" dof="7" vision="true" robot_ip="192.168.1.10" username="admin" password="admin" port="10000" port_realtime="10001" session_inactivity_timeout_ms="60000" connection_inactivity_timeout_ms="2000" use_internal_bus_gripper_comm="true" sim_gazebo="false" sim_isaac="false" prefix="" use_fake_hardware="false" initial_positions="${initial_positions}" use_external_cable="true" fake_sensor_commands="false" gripper_com_port=""> <origin xyz="0 rpy="0 </xacro:load_robot> </robot>

name: kortex_driver
description: ROS2 driver package for the Kinova Robot Hardware.
maintainers: Alex Moriarty, Martin Leroux, Marq Rasmussen
readme: ROS Kortex Driver ROS Kortex driver implements ros2_control hardware interface SystemInterface. Command interfaces driver exports commands interfaces position, velocity, effort interfaces joint defined URDF. Additionally, twist interfaces exported effector operational space control. additional interfaces exported, including set_gripper_max_velocity, set_gripper_max_effort gripper joint, reset_fault/command, reset_fault/async_success fault management. State interfaces driver exports position velocity state interfaces joint defined URDF. Additionally, state interface reset_fault/internal_fault determining robot’s fault state.

readme: ros2doctor folder source code ros2doctor. ROS command line interface tools included standard install ROS distro. ros2doctor similar roswtf ROS examine ROS setup, distribution, platform, network interface, potential issues running ROS system. Usage Run ros2 doctor ros2 wtf(alias) conduct checks. Run ros2 doctor -h/--help print command arguments. Run ros2 doctor -r/--report report checked items. Run ros2 doctor -rf/--report-fail report failed checks Run ros2 doctor -iw/--include-warnings include warnings failed checks. combination. Run ros2 doctor -ep/--exclude-packages exclude package checks report. Add Checks add checks information report, Python entry points add modules setup.py. entry_points={ 'ros2doctor.checks': 'PlatformCheck ros2doctor.api.platform:PlatformCheck', 'NetworkCheck ros2doctor.api.network:NetworkCheck', 'ros2doctor.report': 'PlatformReport ros2doctor.api.platform:PlatformReport', 'NetworkReport ros2doctor.api.network:NetworkReport',

NOTE: startup, gazebo detects publisher /clock, create fully qualified /world/<worldname>/clock topic. Gazebo /clock publisher, sole source clock information. create unidirectional /clock bridge: ros2 run ros_gz_bridge parameter_bridge /clock@rosgraph_msgs/msg/Clock[gz.msgs.Clock alternative set-up bridge override_timestamps_with_wall_time ros parameter set true (default=false). set-up, header timestamps outgoing messages stamped wall time. simulator communicate external system requires wall times. Gazebo Transport talker ROS listener Start parameter bridge watch topics. Shell ~/bridge_ws/install/setup.bash ros2 run ros_gz_bridge parameter_bridge /chatter@std_msgs/msg/String@gz.msgs.StringMsg start ROS listener. Shell /opt/ros/rolling/setup.bash ros2 topic echo /chatter start Gazebo Transport talker. Shell topic /chatter gz.msgs.StringMsg 'data:"Hello"' ROS talker Gazebo Transport listener Start parameter bridge watch topics. Shell ~/bridge_ws/install/setup.bash ros2 run ros_gz_bridge parameter_bridge /chatter@std_msgs/msg/String@gz.msgs.StringMsg start Gazebo Transport listener. Shell topic /chatter start ROS talker. Shell /opt/ros/rolling/setup.bash ros2 topic pub /chatter std_msgs/msg/String "data: 'Hi'" Run bridge exchange images generate Gazebo Transport images Gazebo Sim, converted ROS images, visualized rqt_image_viewer. start Gazebo Sim (don’t forget hit play, Gazebo Sim won’t generate images). Shell sim sensors_demo.sdf Let’s topic camera images published. Shell topic grep image /rgbd_camera/depth_image /rgbd_camera/image start parameter bridge previous topic. Shell ~/bridge_ws/install/setup.bash ros2 run ros_gz_bridge parameter_bridge /rgbd_camera/image@sensor_msgs/msg/Image@gz.msgs.Image start ROS GUI: Shell /opt/ros/rolling/setup.bash ros2 run rqt_image_view rqt_image_view /rgbd_camera/image current images rqt_image_view coming Gazebo (published Gazebo Msgs Gazebo Transport). screenshot shows shell windows expected content ROS Galactic Gazebo Fortress): Static bridge run executable starts bidirectional bridge specific topic message type. static_bridge executable installed bridge. example’s code ros_gz_bridge/src/static_bridge.cpp. code, bridge hardcoded bridge string messages published /chatter topic. Let’s give starting Gazebo ROS terminal start bridge: ros2 run ros_gz_bridge static_bridge terminal start ROS listener: ros2 topic echo /chatter std_msgs/msg/String terminal publish Gazebo message: topic /chatter gz.msgs.StringMsg 'data:"Hello"' point, ROS listener echoing message. let’s ROS Gazebo. terminal start Gazebo listener: topic /chatter terminal publish ROS message: ros2 topic pub /chatter std_msgs/msg/String 'data: "Hello"' Gazebo listener echoing message. Service bridge ROS service requests Gazebo. Let’s unpausing simulation. terminal start service bridge: ros2 run ros_gz_bridge parameter_bridge /world/shapes/control@ros_gz_interfaces/srv/ControlWorld terminal start Gazebo, paused default: sim shapes.sdf terminal ROS request unpause simulation: ros2 service call /world/<world_name>/control ros_gz_interfaces/srv/ControlWorld "{world_control: {pause: false}}" Configuring Bridge YAML configuring topics, easier file-based configuration markup language. case, ros_gz bridge supports YAML file configure parameters. configuration file YAML array maps. configuration bridges showing ways bridge Set topic applies topic_name: "chatter" ros_type_name: "std_msgs/msg/String" gz_type_name: "gz.msgs.StringMsg" Set ROS topic applies ros_topic_name: "chatter_ros" ros_type_name: "std_msgs/msg/String" gz_type_name: "gz.msgs.StringMsg" Set topic applies gz_topic_name: "chatter_gz" ros_type_name: "std_msgs/msg/String" gz_type_name: "gz.msgs.StringMsg" Set topic explicitly ros_topic_name: "chatter_both_ros" gz_topic_name: "chatter_both_gz" ros_type_name: "std_msgs/msg/String" gz_type_name: "gz.msgs.StringMsg" Full set configurations ros_topic_name: "ros_chatter" gz_topic_name: "gz_chatter" ros_type_name: "std_msgs/msg/String" gz_type_name: "gz.msgs.StringMsg" subscriber_queue: Default publisher_queue: Default lazy: true Default "false" direction: BIDIRECTIONAL Default "BIDIRECTIONAL" Bridge directions "GZ_TO_ROS" Bridge topic ROS "ROS_TO_GZ" Bridge ROS topic run bridge node configuration: ros2 run ros_gz_bridge parameter_bridge --ros-args config_file:=$WORKSPACE/ros_gz/ros_gz_bridge/test/config/full.yaml ROS namespace Bridge spawning multiple robots ROS environment, convenient namespaces avoid overlapping topic names. main types namespaces: relative, global private information, refer ROS documentation. Namespaces applied Gazebo topic topic_name gz_topic_name. default, Bridge apply ROS namespace Gazebo topics. enable feature, parameter expand_gz_topic_names. Let’s test topic namespace: Shell ~/bridge_ws/install/setup.bash ros2 run ros_gz_bridge parameter_bridge chatter@std_msgs/msg/String@ignition.msgs.StringMsg --ros-args expand_gz_topic_names:=true __ns:=/demo start Gazebo Transport listener. Shell topic /demo/chatter start ROS talker. Shell /opt/ros/rolling/setup.bash ros2 topic pub /demo/chatter std_msgs/msg/String "data: 'Hi namespace'" changing chatter /chatter ~/chatter obtain results. API ROS Parameters: subscription_heartbeat Period node checks subscribers lazy bridges. config_file YAML file loaded bridge configuration expand_gz_topic_names Enable disable ROS namespace applied topics.

readme: ROS Gazebo Sim package convenient integrate ROS Gazebo, Launch files ROS-enabled executables Run Gazebo Sim convenient launch file, ros2 launch ros_gz_sim gz_sim.launch.py gz_args:="shapes.sdf" Spawn entities create executable spawn SDF URDF entities file disk Gazebo Fuel ROS parameter start Gazebo Sim: ros2 launch ros_gz_sim gz_sim.launch.py spawn model: ros2 run ros_gz_sim create -world default -file 'https://fuel.gazebosim.org/1.0/openrobotics/models/Gazebo' options ros2 run ros_gz_sim create --helpshort <gazebo_ros> export model paths package.xml <gazebo_ros> tag <export> tag package.xml file add paths GZ_SIM_RESOURCE_PATH GZ_SIM_SYSTEM_PLUGIN_PATH, environment variables configure Gazebo search paths resources SDFormat files, meshes, plugins values attributes gazebo_model_path gazebo_media_path appended GZ_SIM_RESOURCE_PATH. plugin_path appended GZ_SIM_SYSTEM_PLUGIN_PATH. Finding resources tutorial learn environment variables. keyword ${prefix} setting values expanded package’s share path ros2 pkg prefix --share <package <export> <gazebo_ros gazebo_model_path="${prefix}/models"/> <gazebo_ros gazebo_media_path="${prefix}/media"/> <gazebo_ros plugin_path="${prefix}/plugins"/> </export> required directory installed CMakeLists.txt install(DIRECTORY models DESTINATION share/${PROJECT_NAME}) order reference models ROS package unambiguously, recommended set gazebo_model_path parent prefix. <export> <gazebo_ros gazebo_model_path="${prefix}/../"/> </export> ROS package called my_awesome_pkg SDFormat model cool cool_robot: my_awesome_pkg models cool_robot model.config model.sdf package.xml gazebo_model_path="${prefix}/../ set reference cool_robot model world file package uri: <sdf version="1.6"> <world name="default"> <include> <uri>package://my_awesome_pkg/models/cool_robot</uri> </include> </world> </sdf> set gazebo_model_path=${prefix}/models, reference cool_robot package://cool_robot, conflict models system.

name: ros_gz_sim_demos
description: Demos using Gazebo Sim simulation with ROS.
readme: ROS Gazebo Sim demos package demos showing Gazebo Sim ROS. Run Gazebo Sim convenient launch file, ros2 launch ros_gz_sim gz_sim.launch.py gz_args:="shapes.sdf" Air pressure Publishes fluid pressure readings. ros2 launch ros_gz_sim_demos air_pressure.launch.xml demo shows custom QoS parameters. sensor data published “best-effort”, subscribe “reliable” data won’t work. difference ros2 topic echo /air_pressure --qos-reliability best_effort ros2 topic echo /air_pressure --qos-reliability reliable Battery current state battery. ros2 launch ros_gz_sim_demos battery.launch.py send command vehicle moves drains battery. ros2 topic pub /model/vehicle_blue/cmd_vel geometry_msgs/msg/Twist "{linear: angular: Camera Publishes RGB camera image info. Images exposed ROS ros_gz_bridge ros_gz_image. image bridge (unidirectional, image_transport): ros2 launch ros_gz_sim_demos image_bridge.launch.py regular bridge: ros2 launch ros_gz_sim_demos camera.launch.xml camera publishes information triggered: ros2 launch ros_gz_sim_demos triggered_camera.launch.xml Trigger camera: ros2 topic pub /camera/trigger std_msgs/msg/Bool "{data: true}" Diff drive Send commands differential drive vehicle listen odometry. ros2 launch ros_gz_sim_demos diff_drive.launch.xml send command ros2 topic pub /model/vehicle_blue/cmd_vel geometry_msgs/msg/Twist "{linear: angular: demo shows custom QoS parameters. commands subscribed “reliable”, publish “best-effort” commands won’t work. difference ros2 topic pub /model/vehicle_blue/cmd_vel geometry_msgs/msg/Twist "{linear: angular: --qos-reliability reliable ros2 topic pub /model/vehicle_blue/cmd_vel geometry_msgs/msg/Twist "{linear: angular: --qos-reliability best_effort Depth camera Depth camera data obtained sensor_msgs/msg/Image, ros_gz_bridge ros_gz_image sensor_msgs/msg/PointCloud2, ros_gz_point_cloud image bridge (unidirectional, image_transport): ros2 launch ros_gz_sim_demos image_bridge.launch.py image_topic:=/depth_camera TODO: Blocked ros_gz_point_cloud issue. Gazebo Sim plugin: ros2 launch ros_gz_sim_demos depth_camera.launch.py GPU lidar GPU lidar data obtained sensor_msgs/msg/LaserScan, ros_gz_bridge sensor_msgs/msg/PointCloud2, ros_gz_bridge ros_gz_point_cloud bridge: ros2 launch ros_gz_sim_demos gpu_lidar_bridge.launch.xml TODO: Blocked ros_gz_point_cloud issue. Gazebo Sim plugin: ros2 launch ros_gz_sim_demos gpu_lidar.launch.py IMU Publishes IMU readings. ros2 launch ros_gz_sim_demos imu.launch.xml TODO: IMU display missing RViz2 Magnetometer Publishes magnetic field readings. ros2 launch ros_gz_sim_demos magnetometer.launch.xml GNSS Publishes satellite navigation readings, Fortress GNSS information obtained sensor_msgs/msg/NavSatFix ros2 launch ros_gz_sim_demos navsat.launch.xml gps_msgs/msg/GPSFix ros2 launch ros_gz_sim_demos navsat_gpsfix.launch.xml RGBD camera RGBD camera data obtained sensor_msgs/msg/Image, ros_gz_bridge ros_gz_image sensor_msgs/msg/PointCloud2, ros_gz_bridge ros_gz_point_cloud image bridge (unidirectional, image_transport): RGB image ros2 launch ros_gz_sim_demos image_bridge.launch.py image_topic:=/rgbd_camera/image Depth image ros2 launch ros_gz_sim_demos image_bridge.launch.py image_topic:=/rgbd_camera/depth_image regular bridge: ros2 launch ros_gz_sim_demos rgbd_camera_bridge.launch.xml TODO: Blocked ros_gz_point_cloud issue. Gazebo Sim plugin: ros2 launch ros_gz_sim_demos rgbd_camera.launch.py Robot description publisher Leverage robot description publisher spawn urdf model gazebo show rviz2. demo launch: ros2 launch ros_gz_sim_demos robot_description_publisher.launch.py Joint States Publisher Publishes joint states robot. demo launch: ros2 launch ros_gz_sim_demos joint_states.launch.py Bridging joint state pose publishers launch file demonstrates bridging Gazebo poses TFMessage visualize pose transforms robot rviz. demo launch: ros2 launch ros_gz_sim_demos tf_bridge.launch.xml

Installation ROS2 Humble Humble Rolling Binary Installation Binary installation Source ROS installation, run: sudo apt install ros-${ROS_DISTRO}-ros-image-to-qimage Source Installation Alternatively build source, source ROS installation, run ROS workspace: ROS Iron Rolling git clone https://github.com/ros-sports/ros_image_to_qimage.git src/ros_image_to_qimage colcon build ROS Humble git clone https://github.com/ros-sports/ros_image_to_qimage.git src/ros_image_to_qimage --branch humble colcon build Usage #include "ros_image_to_qimage/ros_image_to_qimage.hpp" void imageCallback(const sensor_msgs::ImageConstPtr& msg) QImage qImage ros_image_to_qimage::Convert(*msg); package.xml, add <depend>ros_image_to_qimage</depend> CMakeLists.txt, suppose link my_target library: find_package(ros_image_to_qimage REQUIRED) ament_target_dependencies(my_target ros_image_to_qimage) Python ros_image_to_qimage import ros_image_to_qimage def image_callback(self, msg): qimage ros_image_to_qimage.convert(msg) package.xml, add <exec_depend>ros_image_to_qimage</exec_depend>

Compression Note opt select compression benchmarking, generated data random representative specific case. publish non-random data, modify ByteProducer. Number publisher threads case benchmark_publishers binary, pool threads created run publishers. default, number threads equal number publishers. change number threads optional number_of_threads parameter. Building build package rosbag2 build process, turn BUILD_ROSBAG2_BENCHMARKS flag colcon build --cmake-args -DBUILD_ROSBAG2_BENCHMARKS=1) built rosbag2, packages-select option build benchmarks. colcon build --packages-select rosbag2_performance_benchmarking --cmake-args -DBUILD_ROSBAG2_BENCHMARKS=1. General knowledge: benchmarking Background: benchmarking disk writes system understand limitation disk poses throughput data recording. Performance bag write higher extended period time memory). command if=/dev/zero of=/tmp/output conv=fdatasync bs=384k count=1k; /tmp/output method great benchmarking disk easy start requires dependencies. write zeros /tmp/output file block size blocks, ends write finishes. benchmark disk bags written (check mount points change “/tmp/output” path needed). Note: depends parameters running system give ballpark figure ran times. fio sophisticated accurate benchmarks, fio command. big data blocks fio TEST --eta-newline=5s --filename=fio-tempfile.dat --rw=write --size=500m --io_size=10g --blocksize=1024k --ioengine=libaio --fsync=10000 --iodepth=32 --direct=1 --numjobs=1 --runtime=60 --group_reporting. description: RT Manipulators C++ Library
readme: RTマニピュレータC++ライブラリ build_install_library.bash ./build_install_library.bash compiler identification GNU CXX compiler identification GNU Check working compiler: /usr/bin/cc Check working compiler: /usr/bin/cc works Detecting compiler ABI info Detecting compiler ABI info Detecting compile features Built target rt_manipulators_cpp Built target rt_manipulators_cpp Install project... Install configuration: Installing: /usr/local/lib/librt_manipulators_cpp.so.1.0.0 Installing: /usr/local/lib/librt_manipulators_cpp.so.1 Installing: /usr/local/lib/librt_manipulators_cpp.so Up-to-date: /usr/local/include/rt_manipulators_cpp Installing: /usr/local/include/rt_manipulators_cpp/hardware.hpp Installing: /usr/local/include/rt_manipulators_cpp/joint.hpp ビルドに成功すると、CMakeのデフォルトインストールディレクトリ(例：/usr/local/lib、/usr/local/include)に共有ライブラリ(librt_manipulators_cpp.so)とヘッダーファイル(rt_manipulators_cpp/*.hpp)がインストールされます。 test.cpp -lrt_manipulators_cpp uninstall_library.bash ./uninstall_library.bash /usr/local/lib/librt_manipulators_cpp.so.1.0.0 /usr/local/lib/librt_manipulators_cpp.so.1 /usr/local/lib/librt_manipulators_cpp.so /usr/local/include/rt_manipulators_cpp/hardware.hpp /usr/local/include/rt_manipulators_cpp/joint.hpp ライブラリの使い方はサンプル集のREADME.mdを参照してください。 hardware.hpp/cpp Hardwareクラスを実装しています hardware_communicator.hpp/cpp Hardwareクラスのうち、Dynamixelとの通信機能を実装しています hardware_joints.hpp/cpp Hardwareクラスのうち、ジョイント情報を扱う機能を実装しています joints.hpp/cpp config_file_parser.hpp/cpp dynamixel_* 各Dynamixelと通信するためのデータ変換を担います kinematics.hpp/cpp kinematics_utils.hpp/cpp link.hpp GoogleTestのインストール mkdir ~/gtest ~/gtest curl https://github.com/google/googletest/archive/release-1.11.0.tar.gz tar -xvf release-1.11.0.tar.gz mkdir googletest-release-1.11.0/build googletest-release-1.11.0/build cmake sudo install ./run_test_library.bash compiler identification GNU CXX compiler identification GNU Check working compiler: /usr/bin/cc Check working compiler: /usr/bin/cc works Detecting compiler ABI info Start JointTest.initialize_id Test JointTest.initialize_id Passed sec tests passed, tests failed Total Test time (real) sec

Lifecycle Node start driver lifecycle launch file started. driver work correctly, sensor host defined. parameters passed sensor arguments launch file. ros2 launch sick_safetyscanners2 sick_safetyscanners2_lifecycle_launch.py start driver launch file defined parameters. configure activate lifecycle node commands issued: Configure ros2 lifecycle set /sick_safetyscanners2_lifecycle_node configure //Activate ros2 lifecycle set /sick_safetyscanners2_lifecycle_node activate Visualization visualize data start rviz2 subscribe ~/scan topic. default frame_id “scan” customized launch file rviz2 Troubleshooting Check sensor power connected host. Check sensor host subnet Check launch file called correct parameters IP-addresses ports. Services ~/field_data Returns configured protective warning fields sensor ROS2 parameters Parameter Type Default Required startup Information sensor_ip String Sensor address. passed argument launch file. host_ip String Host address. passed argument launch file. interface_ip String Interface address receiving host computer, set host multicast range. default undefined address return error name: sick_safevisionary_driver
description: Provides an interface to read the sensor output of a SICK Safevisionary sensor in ROS 2
maintainers: Marvin Grosse Besselmann, Stefan Scherzinger, Jochen Lienhart
readme: Sick Safevisionary Driver official ROS2 driver Sick safeVisionary2 cameras. top-level readme started. Lifecycle states driver implements thin ROS2 wrapper sick_safevisionary_base library form lifecycle node. contrast conventional ROS2 nodes, lifecycle nodes give control driver’s individual states. handy performing clean reset runtime. You’ll find information individual states state transitions design article. driver’s behavior roughly State Behavior Unconfigured topics advertised previously advertised topics removed. Inactive driver establishes UDP data connection camera processes sensor data publishing. Active driver continuously publishes camera data consistent time stamp topics. Finalized driver shutdown resources cleaned previously advertised topics removed. Managing lifecycle ROS2 command line interface trigger state transitions. commands /sick_safevisionary node primary states: ros2 lifecycle set /sick_safevisionary configure ros2 lifecycle set /sick_safevisionary activate ros2 lifecycle set /sick_safevisionary deactivate ros2 lifecycle set /sick_safevisionary shutdown integration tests Python.

name: system_modes
description:
The system modes concept assumes that a robotics system is built
    from components with a lifecycle. It adds a notion of (sub-)systems,
    hiararchically grouping these nodes, as well as a notion of modes
    that determine the configuration of these nodes and (sub-)systems in
    terms of their parameter values.

name: teleop_twist_joy
description: Generic joystick teleop for twist robots.
readme: ros2/teleop_twist_joy Overview purpose package provide generic facility tele-operating Twist-based ROS robots standard joystick. converts joy messages velocity commands. node rate limiting autorepeat functionality. expected advantage features built joy Executables package teleop_node republishes sensor_msgs/msg/Joy messages scaled geometry_msgs/msg/Twist messages. message type changed geometry_msgs/msg/TwistStamped publish_stamped_twist parameter. Subscribed Topics joy (sensor_msgs/msg/Joy) Joystick messages translated velocity commands. Published Topics cmd_vel (geometry_msgs/msg/Twist geometry_msgs/msg/TwistStamped) Command velocity messages arising Joystick commands. Parameters require_enable_button (bool, default: true) require enable button enabling movement. enable_button (int, default: Joystick button enable regular-speed movement. enable_turbo_button (int, default: Joystick button enable high-speed movement (disabled axis_linear.<axis> Joystick axis linear movement control. axis_linear.x (int, default: axis_linear.y (int, default: axis_linear.z (int, default: scale_linear.<axis> Scale apply joystick linear axis regular-speed movement. scale_linear.x (double, default: scale_linear.y (double, default: scale_linear.z (double, default: scale_linear_turbo.<axis> Scale apply joystick linear axis high-speed movement. scale_linear_turbo.x (double, default: scale_linear_turbo.y (double, default: scale_linear_turbo.z (double, default: axis_angular.<axis> Joystick axis angular movement control. axis_angular.yaw (int, default: axis_angular.pitch (int, default: axis_angular.roll (int, default: scale_angular.<axis> Scale apply joystick angular axis. scale_angular.yaw (double, default: scale_angular.pitch (double, default: scale_angular.roll (double, default: scale_angular_turbo.<axis> Scale apply joystick angular axis high-speed movement. scale_angular_turbo.yaw (double, default: scale_angular_turbo.pitch (double, default: scale_angular_turbo.roll (double, default: inverted_reverse (bool, default: false) invert turning left-right reversing differential wheeled robots). publish_stamped_twist (bool, default: false) publish geometry_msgs/msg/TwistStamped command velocity messages. frame (string, default: 'teleop_twist_joy') Frame header TwistStamped messages. Usage Install users building source required, execute apt-get install ros-<rosdistro>-teleop-twist-joy install. Run launch file arguments changed terminal launch file. configure node match joystick config file common package (atk3, ps3-holonomic, xbox, located https://github.com/ros2/teleop_twist_joy/tree/rolling/config. default, run config xbox) ros2 launch teleop_twist_joy teleop-launch.py joy_config:='xbox' Note: launch file launches joy node run separately. Arguments joy_config (string, default: 'ps3') Config file joy_dev (string, default: Joystick device config_filepath (string, default: '/opt/ros/<rosdistro>/share/teleop_twist_joy/config/' LaunchConfig('joy_config') '.config.yaml') Path config files publish_stamped_twist (bool, default: false) publish geometry_msgs/msg/TwistStamped command velocity messages.

name: autoware_utils
description: The autoware_utils package
maintainers: Jian Kang, Ryohsuke Mitsudome, Esteve Fernandez, Yutaka Kondo
readme: autoware_utils Library Overview autoware_utils library comprehensive toolkit designed facilitate development autonomous driving applications. library essential utilities geometry, mathematics, ROS (Robot Operating System) expansions, diagnostics, extensively Autoware project handle common tasks geometric calculations, data normalization, message conversions, performance monitoring, point cloud transformations. Design Geometry Module geometry module classes functions handling points, vectors, polygons, performing geometric operations: boost_geometry.hpp: Integrates Boost.Geometry advanced geometric computations, defining point, segment, box, linestring, ring, polygon types. alt_geometry.hpp: Implements alternative geometric types operations vectors polygons, including vector arithmetic, polygon creation, geometric predicates. ear_clipping.hpp: algorithms triangulating polygons ear clipping method. gjk_2d.hpp: Implements GJK algorithm fast intersection detection convex polygons. sat_2d.hpp: Implements SAT (Separating Axis Theorem) algorithm detecting intersections convex polygons. random_concave_polygon.hpp random_convex_polygon.hpp: Generate random concave convex polygons testing purposes. pose_deviation.hpp: Calculates deviations poses terms lateral, longitudinal, yaw angles. boost_polygon_utils.hpp: Utility functions manipulating polygons, including: Checking polygon clockwise. Rotating polygons origin. Converting poses shapes polygons. Expanding polygons offset. geometry.hpp: Comprehensive geometric operations, including: Distance calculations points segments. Curvature computation. Pose transformations interpolations. Intersection checks convex polygons GJK. Conversion coordinate systems. Math Module math module offers variety mathematical utilities: accumulator.hpp: class accumulating statistical data, supporting min, max, calculations. constants.hpp: Defines commonly mathematical constants gravity. normalization.hpp: Functions normalizing angles degrees. range.hpp: Functions generating sequences numbers (arange, linspace). trigonometry.hpp: Optimized trigonometric functions faster computation. unit_conversion.hpp: Functions converting units degrees radians, km/h ROS Module ROS module utilities working ROS messages nodes: debug_publisher.hpp: helper class publishing debug messages timestamps. diagnostics_interface.hpp: interface publishing diagnostic messages. logger_level_configure.hpp: Utility configuring logger levels dynamically. managed_transform_buffer.hpp: managed buffer handling static dynamic transforms. marker_helper.hpp: Helper functions creating manipulating visualization markers. msg_covariance.hpp: Indices accessing covariance matrices ROS messages. msg_operation.hpp: Overloaded operators quaternion messages. parameter.hpp: Simplifies parameter retrieval declaration. polling_subscriber.hpp: subscriber class polling policies (latest, newest, processing_time_publisher.hpp: Publishes processing times diagnostic messages. published_time_publisher.hpp: Tracks publishes time messages published. self_pose_listener.hpp: Listens self-pose vehicle. transform_listener.hpp: Manages transformation listeners. update_param.hpp: Updates parameters remote nodes. uuid_helper.hpp: Utilities generating managing UUIDs. wait_for_param.hpp: Waits parameters remote nodes. debug_traits.hpp: Traits identifying debug message types. pcl_conversion.hpp: Efficient conversion transformation PointCloud2 messages PCL point clouds. System Module system module low-level utilities performance monitoring error handling: backtrace.hpp: Prints backtraces debugging. lru_cache.hpp: Implements LRU cache. stop_watch.hpp: Measures elapsed time profiling. time_keeper.hpp: Tracks reports processing time functions. Transform Module Efficient methods transforming manipulating point clouds. Usage Including Headers Autoware Utils library project, include headers top source files: #include "autoware_utils/geometry/boost_geometry.hpp" #include "autoware_utils/math/accumulator.hpp" #include "autoware_utils/ros/debug_publisher.hpp" include autoware_utils/autoware_utils.hpp features: #include "autoware_utils/autoware_utils.hpp" Code Snippets Vector2d alt_geometry.hpp #include "autoware_utils/geometry/alt_geometry.hpp" namespace autoware_utils::alt; int main() Vector2d vec1(3.0, Vector2d vec2(1.0, Compute dot product double dot_product vec1.dot(vec2); Compute norm double norm vec1.norm(); return Accumulator accumulator.hpp #include "autoware_utils/math/accumulator.hpp" int main() autoware_utils::Accumulator<double> acc; acc.add(1.0); acc.add(2.0); acc.add(3.0); std::cout acc.mean() std::cout "Min: acc.min() std::cout "Max: acc.max() std::cout "Count: acc.count() return Detailed Usage Examples Transform Point Clouds ManagedTransformBuffer #include "autoware_utils/ros/managed_transform_buffer.hpp" #include "sensor_msgs/msg/point_cloud2.hpp" #include <rclcpp/rclcpp.hpp> int main(int argc, char argv[]) rclcpp::init(argc, argv); auto node rclcpp::Node::make_shared("transform_node"); Initialize ManagedTransformBuffer autoware_utils::ManagedTransformBuffer transform_buffer(node, false); Load point cloud data sensor_msgs::msg::PointCloud2 cloud_in; Assume populated data sensor_msgs::msg::PointCloud2 cloud_out; Transform point cloud "base_link" "map" frame (transform_buffer.transform_pointcloud("map", cloud_in, cloud_out)) RCLCPP_INFO(node->get_logger(), "Point cloud transformed successfully."); RCLCPP_ERROR(node->get_logger(), "Failed transform point cloud."); rclcpp::shutdown(); return Update Parameters Dynamically update_param.hpp #include "autoware_utils/ros/update_param.hpp" #include <rclcpp/rclcpp.hpp> int main(int argc, char argv[]) rclcpp::init(argc, argv); auto node rclcpp::Node::make_shared("param_node"); double param_value std::vector<rclcpp::Parameter> params node->get_parameters({"my_param"}); (autoware_utils::update_param(params, "my_param", param_value)) RCLCPP_INFO(node->get_logger(), "Updated parameter param_value); RCLCPP_WARN(node->get_logger(), "Parameter 'my_param' rclcpp::shutdown(); return Logging Processing Times ProcessingTimePublisher #include "autoware_utils/ros/processing_time_publisher.hpp" #include <rclcpp/rclcpp.hpp> #include <map> int main(int argc, char argv[]) rclcpp::init(argc, argv); auto node rclcpp::Node::make_shared("processing_time_node"); Initialize ProcessingTimePublisher autoware_utils::ProcessingTimePublisher processing_time_pub(node.get(), "~/debug/processing_time_ms"); Simulate processing times std::map<std::string, double> processing_times {"node1", {"node2", {"node3", Publish processing times processing_time_pub.publish(processing_times); rclcpp::shutdown(); return Manipulating Polygons boost_polygon_utils.hpp #include "autoware_utils/geometry/boost_polygon_utils.hpp" #include "autoware_utils/geometry/boost_geometry.hpp" #include <rclcpp/rclcpp.hpp> int main(int argc, char argv[]) rclcpp::init(argc, argv); auto node rclcpp::Node::make_shared("polygon_node"); Create polygon autoware_utils::Polygon2d polygon; Assume polygon populated points Rotate polygon degrees autoware_utils::Polygon2d rotated_polygon autoware_utils::rotate_polygon(polygon, M_PI Expand polygon offset autoware_utils::Polygon2d expanded_polygon autoware_utils::expand_polygon(polygon, Check polygon clockwise bool is_clockwise autoware_utils::is_clockwise(polygon); rclcpp::shutdown(); return Efficient Point Cloud Conversion pcl_conversion.hpp #include "autoware_utils/ros/pcl_conversion.hpp" #include "sensor_msgs/msg/point_cloud2.hpp" #include <pcl/point_types.h> #include <pcl/PCLPointCloud2.h> #include <Eigen/Core> #include <rclcpp/rclcpp.hpp> int main(int argc, char argv[]) rclcpp::init(argc, argv); auto node rclcpp::Node::make_shared("pcl_conversion_node"); Load point cloud data sensor_msgs::msg::PointCloud2 cloud_in; Assume populated data pcl::PointCloud<pcl::PointXYZ> pcl_cloud; Define transformation matrix Eigen::Matrix4f transform Eigen::Matrix4f::Identity(); Populate transform matrix actual values Convert transform point cloud autoware_utils::transform_point_cloud_from_ros_msg(cloud_in, pcl_cloud, transform); rclcpp::shutdown(); return Handling Debug Message Types debug_traits.hpp #include "autoware_utils/ros/debug_publisher.hpp" #include "autoware_utils/ros/debug_traits.hpp" #include <rclcpp/rclcpp.hpp> int main(int argc, char argv[]) rclcpp::init(argc, argv); auto node rclcpp::Node::make_shared("debug_node"); Initialize DebugPublisher autoware_utils::DebugPublisher debug_pub(node, "/debug"); Publish debug message custom type float debug_data debug_pub.publish<autoware_internal_debug_msgs::msg::Float32Stamped>("example", debug_data); rclcpp::shutdown(); return

name: camera_ros
description: node for libcamera supported cameras (V4L2, Raspberry Pi Camera Modules)
readme: ROS node libcamera ROS node support variety cameras libcamera. node supports Raspberry cameras. Installation Binary packages ROS package repository Linux ROS distributions (check rosdep resolve camera_ros). install DEB RPM packages source ROS distribution source /opt/ros/$ROS_DISTRO/setup.bash DEB package sudo apt install ros-$ROS_DISTRO-camera-ros RPM package sudo dnf install ros-$ROS_DISTRO-camera-ros [!NOTE] installs package libcamera dependency. bloomed version official upstream repo https://git.libcamera.org/libcamera/libcamera.git full support Raspberry camera modules. full camera module support Raspberry build “raspberrypi” fork https://github.com/raspberrypi/libcamera manually. Build Instructions libcamera camera_ros node depends libcamera version ways install dependency: System Package: Linux distributions provide binary libcamera package. Raspberry custom libcamera fork additional support newer camera modules. distribution packages, skip libcamera rosdep key resolving dependencies (rosdep install --skip-keys=libcamera). ROS Package: install newer version ROS repo (package ros-$ROS_DISTRO-libcamera). package installed default building camera_ros source resolving rosdep keys. Source: Finally, build libcamera source. option “raspberrypi” fork Ubuntu. build libcamera part ROS workspace colcon-meson. recommended system-wide installation avoids conflicts system package. install dependencies: Install libcamera build dependencies https://libcamera.org/getting-started.html#dependencies. Install colcon-meson package manager, sudo apt install python3-colcon-meson, pip, pip install colcon-meson. camera_ros camera_ros package built libcamera colcon workspace: create workspace mkdir ~/camera_ws/src ~/camera_ws/src check libcamera sudo apt install python3-colcon-meson Option official upstream git clone https://git.libcamera.org/libcamera/libcamera.git Option raspberrypi fork support newer camera modules git clone https://github.com/raspberrypi/libcamera.git check camera_ros repository git clone https://github.com/christianrauch/camera_ros.git resolve binary dependencies build workspace source /opt/ros/$ROS_DISTRO/setup.bash ~/camera_ws/ rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src --rosdistro $ROS_DISTRO --skip-keys=libcamera colcon build --event-handlers=console_direct+ binary distribution libcamera, skip adding workspace. Additionally, bloomed libcamera package ROS repos, omit --skip-keys=libcamera binary dependency resolved automatically. Launching Node package standalone node executable: ros2 run camera_ros camera_node composable node (camera::CameraNode): ros2 component standalone camera_ros camera::CameraNode launch file composable node: ros2 launch camera_ros camera.launch.py Interfaces camera node interfaces compatible image_pipeline stack. node publishes camera images camera parameters service set camera parameters. Topics type description ~/image_raw sensor_msgs/msg/Image image ~/image_raw/compressed sensor_msgs/msg/CompressedImage image (compressed) ~/camera_info sensor_msgs/msg/CameraInfo camera parameters Services type description ~/set_camera_info sensor_msgs/srv/SetCameraInfo set camera parameters Parameters node sets parameters: static read-only parameters configure camera stream beginning dynamic parameters generated camera controls change per-frame settings changed runtime parameters set command line: standalone executable ros2 run camera_ros camera_node --ros-args param1:=arg1 param2:=arg2 composable node ros2 component standalone camera_ros camera::CameraNode param1:=arg1 param2:=arg2 dynamically ROS parameter API. Dynamic Frame Configuration (controls) dynamic parameters created runtime inspecting controls exposed camera. set exposed controls camera-dependent. ROS parameters formatted exposed controls, parameter names match control names. libcamera expose framerate parameter. framerate range converted duration: \(\text{duration} \frac{1}{\text{framerate}} \cdot set control FrameDurationLimits, exposed camera: set fixed framerate ros2 run camera_ros camera_node --ros-args FrameDurationLimits:="[50000,50000]" Calibration node CameraInfoManager manage camera parameters, camera intrinsics projection distortion coefficients rectification. tasks include loading parameters calibration file ~/.ros/camera_info/$NAME.yaml, publishing ~/camera_info setting parameters service ~/set_camera_info. camera calibrated calibration file exist, node warn user missing file (Camera calibration file ~/.ros/camera_info/$NAME.yaml publish zero-initialised intrinsic parameters. project image plane camera frame rectify image, safely ignore calibrate camera set parameters, cameracalibrator camera_calibration package node interfaces ~/set_camera_info service. Trouble Shooting debug node, compile Debug mode: colcon build --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug run node libcamera ROS debug information gdb: LIBCAMERA_LOG_LEVELS=*:DEBUG ros2 run --prefix 'gdb run --args' camera_ros camera_node --ros-args --log-level debug width:=640 height:=480 Common Issues camera detected (exception cameras standard camera devices, check connection detected (lsusb) detected (v4l2-ctl --list-devices). Raspberry sudo vclog --msg inspect VideoCore log messages detected cameras. vclog part Raspberry Pi’s utils repo https://github.com/raspberrypi/utils. distribution, Ubuntu, build source: install build dependencies sudo apt install --no-install-recommends wget ca-certificates gcc libc6-dev download vclog build wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/raspberrypi/utils/refs/heads/master/vclog/vclog.c gcc vclog.c vclog show VideoCore log messages sudo ./vclog --msg VideoCore log, find camera 'ov5647' camera 'imx708' connected sensor, cases OmniVision Sony IMX708. sensor Raspberry camera modules Hardware Specification match detected sensor camera module. case, OmniVision image sensor belongs Camera Module Sony IMX708 Camera Module Raspberry Camera Module, supported libcamera variant installed. official upstream libcamera supports Camera Module (OmniVision Camera Module (Sony IMX708) supported “raspberrypi” fork libcamera. verify camera working libcamera, build cam libcamera repo list camera devices cam camera OmniVision sensor, show 'ov5647' (/base/axi/pcie@120000/rp1/i2c@88000/ov5647@36) Sony IMX708 'imx708_wide' (/base/axi/pcie@120000/rp1/i2c@88000/imx708@1a). readme: Coal extension Flexible Collision Library FCL forked creating project called HPP-FCL. large part code rewritten removed unused untested part), features developped Due major decided rename HPP-FCL project Coal. Coal develop Simple, (differentiable) efficient simulator robotics high-performance library original FCL library, Coal implements well-established GJK algorithm variants collision detection distance computation. implementations lead state-of-the-art performances, depicted figures hand, benchmarked Coal major software alternatives state art: Bullet simulator, original FCL library Drake framework), libccd library MuJoCo). results depicted figure, notably shows accelerated variants GJK largely outperform large margin times faster). notice y-axis log scale. hand, care dedicated collision detection solvers GJK narrow phase? simply formulate collision detection problem quadratic problem call off-the-shelf optimization solver ProxQP)? observe GJK-based approaches largely outperform solutions based classic optimization solvers solver ProxQP), notably large geometries composed tens hundreds vertices. Open-source projects relying Pinocchio Pinocchio fast flexible implementation Rigid Body Dynamics algorithms analytical derivatives. IfcOpenShell Open source IFC library geometry engine. Crocoddyl software realize model predictive control complex robotics platforms. TSID software implements Task Space Inverse Dynamics HPP SDK implements motion planners humanoids robots. Jiminy simulator based Pinocchio. ocs2 toolbox Optimal Control Switched Systems (OCS2) library python bindings installed simply conda conda-forge install coal. library, include files python bindings installed $CONDA_PREFIX/lib, $CONDA_PREFIX/include $CONDA_PREFIX/lib/python3.XX/site-packages. Coal #include "coal/math/transform.h" #include "coal/mesh_loader/loader.h" #include "coal/BVH/BVH_model.h" #include "coal/collision.h" #include "coal/collision_data.h" #include <iostream> #include <memory> Function load convex mesh `.obj`, `.stl` `.dae` file. function imports object file BVHModel, point cloud hierarchically transformed tree bounding volumes. leaves tree individual points point cloud stored `.obj` file. BVH collision detection. computational efficiency, prefer work convex hull point cloud. insures underlying object convex fast collision detection algorithms GJK EPA called object. creating BVH structure point cloud, function computes convex hull. std::shared_ptr<coal::ConvexBase> loadConvexMesh(const std::string& file_name) coal::NODE_TYPE bv_type coal::BV_AABB; coal::MeshLoader loader(bv_type); coal::BVHModelPtr_t bvh loader.load(file_name); bvh->buildConvexHull(true, return bvh->convex; int main() Create coal shapes. Coal supports primitive shapes: boxes, spheres, capsules, cylinders, ellipsoids, cones, planes, halfspace convex meshes convex hulls clouds points). supports BVHs (bounding volumes hierarchies), height-fields octrees. std::shared_ptr<coal::Ellipsoid> shape1 std::make_shared<coal::Ellipsoid>(0.7, std::shared_ptr<coal::ConvexBase> shape2 loadConvexMesh("../path/to/mesh/file.obj"); Define shapes' placement space coal::Transform3s T1.setQuatRotation(coal::Quaternion3f::UnitRandom()); T1.setTranslation(coal::Vec3s::Random()); coal::Transform3s coal::Transform3s::Identity(); T2.setQuatRotation(coal::Quaternion3f::UnitRandom()); T2.setTranslation(coal::Vec3s::Random()); Define collision requests results. collision request set parameters collision pair. set positive negative security margin. distance shapes security margin, shapes considered collision. name: laser_segmentation
description: Implementation of algorithms for segmentation of laserscans.
readme: laser_segmentation Overview Implementation differents algorithms segmentation laserscans, splitting subsets beams, ROS2 interface. implemented algorithm Jump distance clustering: widely method laser range data mobile robotics. simple fast method segment scans: Euclidean distance adjacent beams exceeds threshold distance, segment generated. Jump distance clustering merge: Similar algorithm checks pre-predecessor segments close deals over-segmented data small cluster outdoor environment. threshold condition implementation mentioned jump distance threshold fixed. dynamic jump distance threshold calculated methods Lee (Lee, Dietmayer (Dietmayer, Santos (Santos, parameters list change Includes dynamic reconfigure server parameter change online configuration algorithms filtering. Laser scan Segments Keywords: ROS2, laser, segmentation, clustering Author: Alberto Tudela laser_segmentation package tested ROS2 Humble Ubuntu research code, expect fitness purpose disclaimed. Installation Building Source Dependencies Robot Operating System (ROS) (middleware robotics), slg_msgs (Library messages interact laser related geometry Humble branch), Building build source, clone latest version main repository colcon workspace compile package colcon_workspace/src git clone https://github.com/ajtudela/laser_segmentation.git humble rosdep install --from-path src --rosdistro humble colcon build --symlink-install Usage scan source running, run laser_segmentation node ros2 launch laser_segmentation segmentation.launch.py Nodes laser_segmentation Segmentation laserscans. Subscribed Topics scan (sensor_msgs/LaserScan) Laser scan topic segmentation performed. Published Topics segments (slg_msgs/SegmentArray) Splitted segments resulting segmentation. segments/visualization (visualization_msgs/MarkerArray) comprises namespaces: “segments”: markers segments showing segments Rviz2. “segments_names”: markers segments. “centroids”: markers segment centroids Rviz2. Parameters scan_topic (string, default: “scan”) Topic laserscan. segment_topic (string, default: “segments”) Topic segmented laser scan. segmentation_type (string, default: “jump_distance”) Choose segmentation algorithms. Jump distance clustering (jump_distance) jump distance merge (jump_distance_merge). Parameters filtering min_points_segment (int, default: Minimium number points segment. max_points_segment (int, default: Maximum number points segment. min_avg_distance_from_sensor (double, default: Minimium average distance sensor. max_avg_distance_from_sensor (double, default: Maximum average distance sensor. min_segment_width (double, default: Minimium width segment. max_segment_width (double, default: Maximum width segment. Parameters segmentation algorithms method_threshold (string, default: Method calculate dynamic jump distance threshold jump_distance jump_distance_merge algorithms. methods based lee (Lee, diet (Dietmayer, santos (Santos, distance_threshold (double, default: algorithms mentioned selected, parameters jump distance segment created. noise_reduction (double, default: Parameter noise reduction “Santos” “Dietmayer” algorithms.

description: Tutorial for point_cloud_transport.
readme: <POINT CLOUD TRANSPORT TUTORIAL> ROS2 Contents Writing Simple Publisher Code Publisher Code Explained Running Publisher Writing Simple Subscriber Code Subscriber Code Explained Running Subscriber Publishers Subscribers Plugins Running Publisher Subsriber Changing Transport Changing Transport Behavior Implementing Custom Plugins Writing Simple Publisher section, create publisher node, opens ROS bag publishes PointCloud2 messages tutorial assumes created workspace [](https://github.com/ros-perception/point_cloud_transport) [](https://github.com/ros-perception/point_cloud_transport_plugins) start, change directory, clone repository, unzip rosbag resources folder: ~/<point_cloud_transport_ws>/src git clone https://github.com/ros-perception/point_cloud_transport_tutorial point_cloud_transport_tutorial tar resources/ -xvf resources/rosbag2_2023_08_05-16_08_51.tar.xz ~/<point_cloud_transport_ws> colcon build --merge-install --event-handlers console_direct+ Code Publisher my_publisher.cpp #include <point_cloud_transport/point_cloud_transport.hpp> reading rosbag #include <ament_index_cpp/get_package_share_directory.hpp> #include <rclcpp/serialization.hpp> #include <rclcpp/rclcpp.hpp> #include <rosbag2_cpp/reader.hpp> #include <rosbag2_cpp/storage_options.hpp> #include <rosbag2_cpp/converter_interfaces/serialization_format_converter.hpp> #include <sensor_msgs/msg/point_cloud2.hpp> int main(int argc, char argv) rclcpp::init(argc, argv); auto node std::make_shared<rclcpp::Node>("point_cloud_publisher"); point_cloud_transport::PointCloudTransport pct(node); point_cloud_transport::Publisher pub pct.advertise("pct/point_cloud", const std::string bagged_cloud_topic "/point_cloud"; const std::string shared_directory ament_index_cpp::get_package_share_directory( "point_cloud_transport_tutorial"); const std::string bag_file shared_directory "/resources/rosbag2_2023_08_05-16_08_51"; boiler-plate rosbag2 read bag rosbag2_storage::StorageOptions storage_options; storage_options.uri bag_file; storage_options.storage_id "mcap"; rosbag2_cpp::ConverterOptions converter_options; converter_options.input_serialization_format "cdr"; converter_options.output_serialization_format "cdr"; open rosbag rosbag2_cpp::readers::SequentialReader reader; reader.open(storage_options, converter_options); sensor_msgs::msg::PointCloud2 cloud_msg; rclcpp::Serialization<sensor_msgs::msg::PointCloud2> cloud_serialization; (reader.has_next() rclcpp::ok()) serialized data auto serialized_message reader.read_next(); rclcpp::SerializedMessage extracted_serialized_msg(*serialized_message->serialized_data); (serialized_message->topic_name bagged_cloud_topic) deserialize convert message cloud_serialization.deserialize_message(&extracted_serialized_msg, &cloud_msg); publish message pub.publish(cloud_msg); rclcpp::spin_some(node); rclcpp::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(100)); reader.close(); node.reset(); rclcpp::shutdown(); Code Publisher Explained break code piece piece. Header including [](https://github.com/ros-perception/point_cloud_transport): #include <point_cloud_transport/point_cloud_transport.hpp> Creates PointCloudTransport instance initializes Node shared pointer. Methods PointCloudTransport create point cloud publishers subscribers similar methods Node create generic publishers subscribers. point_cloud_transport::PointCloudTransport pct(node); PointCloudTransport method create publisher base topic “pct/point_cloud”. Depending plugins built, additional (per-plugin) topics derived base topic advertised. argument size publishing queue. point_cloud_transport::Publisher pub pct.advertise("pct/point_cloud", Publishes sensor_msgs::PointCloud2 message rosbag: sensor_msgs::msg::PointCloud2 cloud_msg; rosbag boiler plate populate cloud_msg publish message pub.publish(cloud_msg); spin node... rclcpp::spin_some(node); repeat... Running Publisher run my_publisher.cpp open terminal root workspace run source install/setup.bash ros2 run point_cloud_transport_tutorial publisher_test Writing Simple Subscriber section, create subscriber node, receives PointCloud2 messages prints number points Code Subscriber my_subscriber.cpp: #include <point_cloud_transport/point_cloud_transport.hpp> #include <rclcpp/rclcpp.hpp> #include <sensor_msgs/msg/point_cloud2.hpp> int main(int argc, char argv) rclcpp::init(argc, argv); auto node std::make_shared<rclcpp::Node>("point_cloud_subscriber"); point_cloud_transport::PointCloudTransport pct(node); point_cloud_transport::Subscriber pct_sub pct.subscribe( "pct/point_cloud", [node](const sensor_msgs::msg::PointCloud2::ConstSharedPtr msg) RCLCPP_INFO_STREAM( node->get_logger(), "Message received, number points msg->width msg->height); RCLCPP_INFO_STREAM(node->get_logger(), "Waiting point_cloud message..."); rclcpp::spin(node); rclcpp::shutdown(); return Code Subscriber Explained break code piece piece. Header including [](https://github.com/ros-perception/point_cloud_transport): #include <point_cloud_transport/point_cloud_transport.hpp> Initializes ROS node: rclcpp::init(argc, argv); auto node rclcpp::Node::make_shared("point_cloud_subscriber"); Creates PointCloudTransport instance initializes Node. Methods PointCloudTransport create point cloud publishers subscribers similar methods NodeHandle create generic publishers subscribers. point_cloud_transport::PointCloudTransport pct(node); PointCloudTransport method create subscriber base topic “pct/point_cloud”. argument size subscribing queue. argument tells subscriber execute lambda function message received. point_cloud_transport::Subscriber pct_sub pct.subscribe( "pct/point_cloud", [node](const sensor_msgs::msg::PointCloud2::ConstSharedPtr msg) RCLCPP_INFO_STREAM( node->get_logger(), "draco message received, number points msg->width msg->height); Select specific transport select specific transport TransportHint class. Creates TransportHint shared pointer. subscriber subscribe transport case “pct/point_cloud/draco”), raw “pct/point_cloud” topic. auto transport_hint std::make_shared<point_cloud_transport::TransportHints>("draco"); PointCloudTransport method create subscriber base topic “pct/point_cloud” add transport_hint variable argument. auto transport_hint std::make_shared<point_cloud_transport::TransportHints>("draco"); point_cloud_transport::Subscriber pct_sub pct.subscribe( "pct/point_cloud", [node](const sensor_msgs::msg::PointCloud2::ConstSharedPtr msg) RCLCPP_INFO_STREAM( node->get_logger(), "draco message received, number points msg->width msg->height); transport_hint.get()); Running Subscriber run my_subscriber.cpp, open terminal root workspace run source install/setup.bash ros2 run point_cloud_transport_tutorial subscriber_test --ros-args point_cloud_transport:=draco point_cloud_transport parameter read point_cloud_transport library. complexity parameters hidden library. Subscriber: plugin decompresses CompressedPointCloud2 topic. "point_cloud_transport/raw" package: point_cloud_transport Publisher: default publisher. publishes PointCloud2 as-is base topic. Subscriber: default pass-through subscriber topics type sensor_msgs/PointCloud2. Shut publisher node restart list published topics [](https://github.com/ros-perception/point_cloud_transport_plugins) installed, /pct/point_cloud/draco [draco_point_cloud_transport/CompressedPointCloud2] publisher ros2 run point_cloud_transport_tutorial my_subscriber --ros-args __node:=draco_listener point_cloud_transport:=<point_cloud_transport_type> ros2 run point_cloud_transport_tutorial my_subscriber --ros-args __node:=draco_listener point_cloud_transport:=draco check node graph rqt_graph Changing Transport Behavior transport, tweak settings compression level speed, quantization attributes point cloud, Transport plugins expose settings rqt_reconfigure. /point_cloud_transport/draco/ change multiple parameters compression fly. let’s adjust position quantization. default, “draco” transport quantization bits, allowing distinquishable positions axis; let’s change bits positions): ros2 run rqt_reconfigure rqt_reconfigure pick /pct/point_cloud/draco drop-down menu move quantization_POSITION slider visualize messages, RVIZ, able level detail point cloud drop. name: reach_ros
description: The reach_ros package
readme: REACH ROS Plugins package ROS2-based plugin implemenations REACH kinematics, evaluation, display interfaces Installation clone repository colcon workspace ~/reach_ws/src git clone https://github.com/ros-industrial/reach_ros2.git Install dependencies vcs import src src/reach_ros2/dependencies.repos rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src Build repository colcon build --symlink-install Demo simple demonstration capability repository demo sub-directory. instructions details launch demo ROS Python instructions Python interface launch studies. Usage steps run reach study robot ROS1 infrastructure plugins. Create files describing robot system required REACH plugins URDF, SRDF, kinematics file, joint limits file, Create mesh model workpiece Note: origin model align kinematic base frame robot Create point cloud target points workpiece point cloud generated command line tool PCL pcl_mesh_sampling <workpiece_mesh>.ply <output_cloud>.pcd -n_samples <number samples> -leaf_size <leaf_size> -write_normals true Create configuration YAML file defining parameters reach study configuration interface plugins demo Run setup launch file ros2 launch reach_ros setup.launch robot_description_file:=<path_to_URDF> Run reach study analysis ros2 launch reach_ros start.launch robot_description_file:=<path_to_URDF> robot_description_semantic_file:=<path_to_SRDF> robot_description_kinematics_file:=<path_to_kinematics.yaml> robot_description_joint_limits_file:=<path_to_joint_limits.yaml> config_file:=<config_file.yaml> config_name:=<arbitrary_config> results_dir:=<arbitrary_results_directory> Evaluation Plugins Manipulability plugin MoveIt! calculate manipulability robot pose. Higher manipulability results higher pose score. Range: inf) Parameters: planning_group planning group evaluate manipulability robot pose jacobian_row_subset (optional) indices rows Jacobian evaluating manipulability. row indices correspond output space jacobian_row_subset: position manipulability Manipulability Scaled plugin MoveIt! calculate manipulability robot pose divided characteristic length motion group. characteristic length computed walking base link tip link motion group summing distances adjacent links. Higher scaled manipulability results higher pose score. Range: inf) Parameters: planning_group planning group evaluate manipulability robot pose jacobian_row_subset (optional) indices rows Jacobian evaluating manipulability. row indices correspond output space jacobian_row_subset: position manipulability excluded_links (optional) names links contained motion group contribute characteristic length Manipulability Ratio plugin MoveIt! calculate manipulability robot pose evaluate score. score calculated ratio smallest manipulability largest manipulability larger ratio, uniform dexterity higher score. Range Parameters: planning_group planning group evaluate manipulability robot pose jacobian_row_subset (optional) indices rows Jacobian evaluating manipulability. row indices correspond output space jacobian_row_subset: position manipulability Distance Penalty plugin MoveIt! collision environment calculate distance closest collision robot pose. distance score robot pose. Larger distance closest collision results higher pose score. Range: Parameters: planning_group planning group solve robot’s inverse kinematics distance_threshold distance closest surfaces collision inverse kinematics solution considered invalid collision_mesh_filename (optional) filename ROS package URI format) reach object mesh collision checking `package:////.stl collision_mesh_frame (optional) frame collision mesh attached left unspecified, collision mesh attached kinematic base frame planning_group touch_links names robot links reach object mesh allowed collide exponent score min(abs(closest_distance_to_collision distance_threshold), 1.0)^exponent. Joint Penalty plugin MoveIt! robot model calculate robot pose score based pose deviates center joint range. Robot poses closer center joint range result higher pose scores. Range: Parameters: planning_group planning_group evaluate joint penalty Solvers MoveIt! Solver plugin MoveIt! kinematics solvers collision checking produce collision aware solutions Parameters: planning_group planning group distance_threshold distance nearest collision invalidate solution. parameter set solutions distance nearest collision invalidated collision_mesh_filename (optional) file path collision mesh model workpiece, package:// ‘file://’ URI format collision_mesh_frame (optional) frame collision mesh attached left unspecified, collision mesh attached kinematic base frame planning_group touch_links links allowed contact collision mesh evaluation_plugin parameters) evaluation plugin score solution poses Discretized MoveIt! Solver plugin performs function MoveIt! solver plugin calculates solutions target discretized Z-axis input angle. pose score returned. Parameters: planning_group planning group distance_threshold distance nearest collision invalidate solution. parameter set solutions distance nearest collision invalidated collision_mesh_filename (optional) file path collision mesh model workpiece, package:// ‘file://’ URI format collision_mesh_frame (optional) frame collision mesh attached left unspecified, collision mesh attached kinematic base frame planning_group touch_links links allowed contact collision mesh evaluation_plugin parameters) evaluation plugin score solution poses discretization_angle angle radians) sample target pose Z-axis Display Plugins ROS Reach Display plugin publishes joint state visualization markers display results reach study. markers interactive: Left-clicking marker change robot position solution works publishing sensor_msgs/JointState message reach_joints topic, source topic joint_state_publisher Right-clicking marker bring context menu displays score reach target markers colorized heat-map, red represents highest scores hottest) blue represents lowest scores coldest). Unreachable points colorized black. methods computing values heat map: default, values heat map computed ratio individual scores maximum score. scores reach study consistent close maximum, colors markers red target maximum score deepest hue red). scores reach study targets differ greatly, variation color method produces meaningful display. values computed “full color range”, scores fully normalized colorization. lowest score displayed deepest hue blue highest score shown deepest hue red. valuable highlighting differences reachability misleading due normalization scores. Parameters: collision_mesh_filename file path collision mesh model workpiece, package:// file:// URI format collision_mesh_frame frame collision mesh attached left unspecified, collision mesh attached kinematic_base_frame kinematic_base_frame base frame kinematic tree display interactive markers marker_scale length meters) arrow markers representing target Cartesian points use_full_color_range (optional, default: False) Colorize heat map full range colors target lowest score deepest hue blue, target highest score deepest hue red) hue_low_score (optional, default: changing default heatmap style coloring color scale HSV space hue_low_score: hue_high_score: lead red green color scale). hue_high_score (optional, default: hue_low_score explanation. Target Pose Generator Plugins Transformed Point Cloud Target Pose Generator plugin inherits reach::PointCloudTargetPoseGenerator, creates target reach study waypoints point cloud file normals, transforms point cloud points desired URDF frame (typically kinematic base frame robot). Parameters: pcd_file path point cloud file (.pcd format), package:// file:// URI points_frame frame URDF point cloud points relative origin frame point cloud) target_frame frame point cloud points transformed reach study purposes. kinematic base frame robot

name: soar_ros
description: An interface library to connect ROS2 with the Soar cognitive architecture.
readme: soar_ros: ROS Interface Soar ROS2 package interface Soar cognitive architecture creating wrappers ROS2 messages handling Soar kernel continuos mode. Soar cognitive architecture developed University Michigan. field cognitive robotics projects, e.g. a drone robot. integration Soar ROS difficult complex projects, include multiple publishers, subscribers, services clients. main limitation orginates synchronous callback model Soar inspired creation wrapper. detailed explanation reason development package read software architecture. package relies forked version Soar. major include cmake-based build scons removal SWIG language interfaces. detailed comparison commit history fork. Features library developed targeting ROS Humble Ubuntu configurations tested. blocking Soar kernel Publisher Subscriber Service Client features supported, Action Server Client Multiple Soar agents Definition description public API API documentation generated rosdoc2, build documentation.
